twitter_thread: 27 productivity apps tested, 23 failed. Only 4 had features that actually improved my workflow.
Eisenhower Matrix with Trello: 30 tasks prioritized in 10 minutes. Asana works too.
Ditched 5 note-taking apps for Evernote. Saved 2 hours/week on syncing alone.
Pomodoro Technique with Tomato Timer: 28% productivity boost in 2 weeks.
Google Calendar for personal, Microsoft Exchange for work. No more double bookings.
2-minute rule: 15 tasks done in 30 minutes. No procrastination.
What's your go-to productivity hack and how much time does it save you? #productivityhacks #software
pack.linkedin: After testing 27 productivity apps, I found that 23 of them were redundant. The remaining 4 had unique features that made a real difference. For instance, using the Eisenhower Matrix with Trello allowed me to prioritize 30 tasks in just 10 minutes. I also simplified my note-taking by ditching 5 apps for Evernote, saving 2 hours a week on syncing. What are some of your favorite productivity tools and how have they impacted your workflow?
